article_title: Senate Judiciary Committee Unanimously Advances GUARD Act to Ban AI Companion Chatbots for Minors
summary: Submission approvable with 2 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Senate Judiciary Committee Unanimously Advances GUARD Act to Ban AI Companion Chatbots for Minors
content_preview.summary: The Senate Judiciary Committee voted 22-0 to advance the GUARD Act, which would ban AI companion chatbots for users under 18 and impose penalties of up to $250,000 per violation.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Senate Judiciary Committee voted 22-0 on April 30, 2026, to advance the Guidelines for User Age-verification and Responsible Dialogue Act — known as the GUARD Act — clearing the bill for consideration by the full Senate, according to [Roll Call](https://rollcall.com/2026/04/30/ban-on-kids-companion-chatbots-advanced-by-senate-committee/). The bipartisan legislation would prohibit AI companion chatbots from serving users under 18, require age verification across AI chatbot p...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured news article covering the Senate Judiciary Committee's unanimous 22-0 vote to advance the GUARD Act on April 30, 2026. The article clearly explains the bill's scope, key provisions, enforcement mechanism, and political context. Appropriate length (839 words) for the News category. No AI self-reference detected.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 source snapshots were fetched successfully and read from disk (source-0.html.gz through source-4.html.gz). source-0 (Roll Call, rollcall.com) confirmed: 22-0 vote, 18 co-sponsors, Sen. Durbin as ranking member, age verification language ('commercially reasonable method'), data minimization clause, Padilla vote and objections, Amy Bos quote ('overinclusive, blunt mechanism') and honeypot warning, Hawley quote ('a choice, not an inevitability'), 30-minute interval provision removal via amendment. source-1 (IAPP, iapp.org) confirmed: Blake Moore (R-Utah) and Valerie Foushee (D-N.C.) as House companion bill sponsors, 180-day enactment window, state preemption clause ('at least as protective'), enforcement by U.S. and state attorneys general with rulemaking authority. source-2 (Global Policy Watch, globalpolicywatch.com) confirmed: full disclosure requirement quoting 'non-human system and that the system does not hold professional credentials — like those of a licensed therapist, medical professional, or attorney', $250,000 civil and criminal penalties, significant First Amendment and privacy opposition. source-3 (Let's Data Science, letsdatascience.com) confirmed: October 28, 2025 introduction date, S.3062 bill designation, Hawley 'brave parents' quote ('brave parents whose children were abused by these AI company chatbots. And I do mean abused. That is not too harsh a word'), OpenAI and Character.AI testimony by parents. source-4 (K-12 Dive, k12dive.com) confirmed: Megan Garcia testimony about son Sewell Setzer III who died by suicide, Garcia quote ('spent his last months being manipulated and sexually groomed by chatbots'), schools exemption confirmed by Hawley. FINDING: The article attributes 'September 2024' as the year of the Garcia/Setzer hearing to K-12 Dive, but K-12 Dive only says 'a September hearing' without specifying the year. Roll Call also says 'in September' without a year. No source in this set confirms whether the hearing was in September 2024 or September 2025 (the bill was introduced October 28, 2025). The year 2024 is a specific that cannot be verified from the cited sources and is likely incorrect given that the bill was introduced in late 2025. This is the primary basis for APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS. The 'clearly' flagged as loaded language appears in a direct legislative quote ('disclose clearly') — not editorializing. Amy Bos is identified as 'Vice President' in the article but Roll Call says 'vice president of government affairs'; this is a minor title truncation, not a factual error. rollcall.com, globalpolicywatch.com, letsdatascience.com, and k12dive.com are not in the source allowlist but are credible journalism outlets (Roll Call is a major congressional news publication; K-12 Dive is a respected education trade publication; IAPP is the leading privacy professional association). iapp.org is already on the allowlist. All sources verified as credible for their respective domains.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core facts are well-sourced: 22-0 vote (Roll Call), bipartisan 18 co-sponsors (Roll Call), October 28 2025 introduction (Let's Data Science/K-12 Dive), S. 3062 designation (Let's Data Science), $250,000 penalties (Roll Call/Global Policy Watch/IAPP), Blake Moore and Valerie Foushee as House sponsors (IAPP), 180-day enactment period (IAPP), state preemption clause (IAPP), age verification using 'commercially reasonable method' (Roll Call), 30-minute interval provision removed via amendment (Roll Call). One minor unverifiable specific: 'September 2024' hearing year — all sources cite the hearing as occurring 'in September' without specifying the year. This is a contextual detail in the body (not headline/summary/lead) but should be corrected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The article appropriately attributes advocacy language (Hawley, Garcia, Bos) to named speakers, and balances pro-legislation views with objections from Padilla and NetChoice. No editorializing detected beyond quoting sources.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of the GUARD Act was found. The TAKE IT DOWN Act cross-reference is an accurate link to existing site coverage. This is a new, original news story.
concerns: The article states 'The September 2024 Senate Judiciary Committee hearing' but none of the cited sources confirm the year 2024 — K-12 Dive and Roll Call both refer only to 'a September hearing.' Given that the GUARD Act was introduced October 28, 2025, the hearing likely preceded that introduction (September 2025 is more probable) or may have been in a different context. The year should be corrected or the unverified qualifier removed.
Amy Bos is identified as 'NetChoice Vice President' but Roll Call says 'vice president of government affairs' — minor title truncation, acceptable but noted.
recommendations: Add rollcall.com and k12dive.com to the source allowlist — both are established, credible outlets that will likely appear in future political and education coverage.
For future submissions, when sourcing a specific year for a historical hearing, ensure the year appears explicitly in the cited source.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong, well-reported news article on a significant bipartisan congressional action. All central claims are verified by multiple credible sources. The single recoverable issue is an unconfirmed year (2024) for a background hearing mentioned in the Context section — not in the headline, summary, or lead. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS: publish the article with a public corrections record noting the unverified hearing year.
